q: The _______ in a camera is a circular opening that controls the amount of light entering the lens.
The image formed on the film of a camera is real, inverted, and _______.
The size or diameter of the aperture is expressed in terms of the _______.
A _______ microscope consists of a single converging lens of short focal length.
In a simple microscope, the object is placed between the focus and the _______.
The image formed by a simple microscope is virtual, erect, and _______.
A compound microscope consists of two converging lenses: the objective and the _______.
The lens near the object in a compound microscope is called the _______ lens.
The final image formed by a compound microscope is virtual and _______ with respect to the original object.
An _______ telescope is used to view very distant objects like stars and planets.
In an astronomical telescope, the objective lens has a _______ focal length than the eyepiece.
When a telescope is in _______ adjustment, the final image is formed at infinity.
The length of an astronomical telescope in normal adjustment is the sum of the _______ of the two lenses.
A _______ telescope produces a final image that is erect with respect to the object.
The _______ telescope uses a diverging lens as the eyepiece to provide an erect image.
The _______ of the human eye acts as a screen where the image is formed.
The process by which the eye changes its focal length to focus on objects at different distances is called _______.
The _______ point is the closest distance at which an eye can see an object clearly.
A person with _______ can see nearby objects clearly but cannot see distant objects clearly.
Myopia is corrected by using a _______ lens.
A person with _______ can see distant objects clearly but cannot see nearby objects clearly.
Hypermetropia is corrected by using a _______ lens.
The unit of power of a lens is the _______.
The power of a lens is the _______ of its focal length in meters.
Spectacles are optical instruments that employ _______.
In a camera, the _______ controls the time for which light falls on the film.
The magnification of a compound microscope is the _______ of the magnifications of the objective and eyepiece.
The distance between the objective and eyepiece in a compound microscope is called the _______.
For a normal eye, the near point distance is approximately _______ cm.
In a myopic eye, the image of a distant object is formed _______ the retina.
In a hypermetropic eye, the image of a nearby object is formed _______ the retina.
The _______ lens in a terrestrial telescope is used to make the final image erect.
A _______ lens has a positive power.
A _______ lens has a negative power.
The _______ is the colored part of the eye that regulates the size of the pupil.
A _______ binocular uses prisms to fold the light path and provide a wider field of view.
The magnifying power of a telescope is the ratio of the focal length of the _______ to that of the eyepiece.
The _______ number is defined as the ratio of focal length to the diameter of the aperture.
When photographing in dim light, a _______ aperture must be used.
A camera’s image is recorded on a film or a digital _______.
The objective of a compound microscope must have a _______ focal length.
The separation of lenses in a Galilean telescope is given by _______.
The _______ defect of vision occurs when the crystalline lens becomes milky and cloudy.
The ability of the eye to see clearly both near and far objects is called its power of _______.
A converging lens used as a magnifier produces an image at the _______ distance of distinct vision.
In a telescope, the _______ gather light from the distant object.
The final image in a simple microscope is _______.
The image formed on the retina is _______.
The _______ nerves carry the image signals from the retina to the brain.
The _______ of a lens system is the reciprocal of its equivalent focal length.
